title: Physurus foliosus
taxonomy: Physurus foliosus is classified as a species of plant within the family Fabaceae. This classification places it in the order Fabales, which includes other legume plants. The family Fabaceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants that are primarily known for their ability to fix nitrogen in the soil. Within the family Fabaceae, Physurus foliosus is also classified as a member of the subfamily Faboideae. This subfamily is further divided into several tribes, with Physurus foliosus belonging to the tribe Millettieae.

The leaves of Physurus foliosus are used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. intro: Physurus foliosus, commonly known as the Indian mulberry or Bael, is a deciduous tree native to the tropical regions of India and Southeast Asia. The tree's bark, leaves, and roots are used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and skin problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Physurus foliosus include flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
